sentiment.label: Restrictive
sentiment.detail: Trump blocks Anthropic, White House pressures states on child safety, 90-day EO deadline looms

sentiment.label: Cautious
sentiment.detail: Federal enforcement actions vs. state resistance; litigation expected

sentiment.label: Mixed
sentiment.detail: EU enforcement active; US federal-state conflict intensifying

risks.factors: Federal preemption EO vs. bipartisan state resistance; DOJ litigation task force active; Anthropic supply-chain designation precedent; March 11 deadline for Commerce evaluation
AI Act prohibited practices enforced Feb 2, 2025; high-risk obligations Aug 2, 2026; penalties 7% turnover
Generative AI regulations, algorithm registration, content alignment requirements
ICO guidance, sector-specific rules via existing regulators, pro-innovation approach

events.title: Trump Orders Federal Agencies to Stop Using Anthropic
events.summary: Trump directed all federal agencies to cease using Anthropic over Pentagon dispute regarding weapons and surveillance guardrails. 6-month phase-out ordered Feb 27. Pentagon designated Anthropic a supply-chain risk. Unprecedented use of federal procurement as enforcement mechanism.

events.title: White House Pressures States on Child Safety AI Bills
events.summary: White House contacted Florida Speaker Daniel Perez to block DeSantis AI Bill of Rights; previously sent letter opposing Utah HB 286. Reports of plans to pressure Tennessee, Nebraska. Administration opposes bills protecting minors from AI-generated explicit images.

events.title: FTC Policy Statement and Disclosure Standard Deadlines Set
events.summary: FTC directed to issue AI policy statement by March 11, 2026 on state law preemption. David Sacks ordered to explore federal AI model disclosure standard by March 16. Expected to frame preemption arguments for DOJ lawsuits.

events.title: States Continue Introducing AI Bills Despite Federal Threats
events.summary: New bills introduced in Georgia (2), Louisiana (2), Minnesota, Rhode Island as of March 2. Connecticut advancing algorithmic pricing amendments. Bipartisan state-level activity continues unabated.

events.title: California Launches First State AI Enforcement Unit
events.summary: California Attorney General Rob Bonta formally launched dedicated AI oversight and accountability program on Feb 17. Actively investigating xAI for non-consensual explicit images.

events.title: Republican Governor Publicly Opposes Federal Preemption
events.summary: Utah Governor Spencer Cox called Trump administration preemption efforts 'preposterous' on Feb 23, joining CO, CA, TX in bipartisan state resistance.

deadlines.title: Connecticut AI Algorithmic Pricing Amendment Hearing
deadlines.description: Hearing on facial recognition, algorithmic pricing, employment profiling, and precise geolocation amendments to CTDPA.

deadlines.title: FTC AI Policy Statement Due
deadlines.description: FTC to issue policy statement describing how FTC Act applies to AI and when state laws are preempted by federal deceptive practices rules.

deadlines.title: Trump EO 90-Day State AI Evaluation Deadline
deadlines.description: Commerce Department must publish evaluation of 'onerous' state AI laws. Expected to trigger DOJ lawsuit targets and BEAD funding withholding decisions.

deadlines.title: Federal AI Model Disclosure Standard Due
deadlines.description: David Sacks directed to explore federal reporting or disclosure standard for AI models that would preempt state requirements.

deadlines.title: Colorado SB 24-205 Implementation
deadlines.description: Consumer Protections for AI Act takes effect (delayed from Feb 1 by SB 25B-004). Likely DOJ preemption challenge.

deadlines.title: EU AI Act High-Risk Obligations
deadlines.description: Full compliance required for high-risk AI systems under Annex III

deadlines.title: EU AI Act Full Application
deadlines.description: Remaining AI Act provisions apply, including high-risk systems in Annex I
